core_payload.content:
# Vault Stewardship Swarm Runtime Modes

## 1. Role
Vault Stewardship Swarm Runtime Modes owns the day and night operating posture of stewardship. It keeps assistant-facing co-pilot behavior, daemon footing, assignment continuity, and operator-visible runtime controls together so stewardship can shift modes without hiding how or why it is currently active.

## 2. Runtime Mode Surface
This sub-hub groups `background-agent-runtime`, `personal-ai-assistant`, `chat-to-capsules`, `agent-instruction-surfaces`, `agent-subagents`, `agent-control-plane`, `agent-daemon`, `agent-async-assignment`, and `night-shift-autonomy`. Together they define how stewardship shows up during the day, persists through the night, receives instructions, routes assignments, and stays visible to operators rather than behaving like silent background magic.

## 3. Operating Boundary
This sub-hub does not own vault reading, critique, validation handoff, or mutation packet design. Those responsibilities belong to `inventory-pressure`, `work-planning-review`, and `mutation-workers` so runtime posture remains separate from judgment and direct graph edits.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Vault Stewardship Swarm Runtime Modes groups the day and night posture surfaces of stewardship: Background Agent Runtime, Personal AI Assistant, Chat to Capsules, instruction surfaces, subagents, control plane, daemon, async assignment, and Night Shift Autonomy. It keeps operator-facing co-pilot behavior and long-lived background continuity together so stewardship can shift modes without hiding how it is currently active. This boundary matters because runtime posture should stay visible and governable rather than silently fused into reading, review, or mutation logic.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted during Dream Vault Stewardship Swarm decomposition to isolate day and night runtime posture, operator surfaces, and daemon continuity from reading, review, and mutation duties.
